description_long: This is a tall, stone tower with a steeply pitched, dark slate roof and four pointed spires at the corners of the upper section. The main body of the tower is constructed from light gray stone blocks, with a prominent vertical section leading up to a clock face on one side. Gothic-style arched windows are present on the upper part of the tower, and a large arched doorway is visible at the base. Attached to the side is a lower, elongated structure with a series of arched openings and a smaller turret at the end, suggesting a chapel or ancillary hall. The overall design is reminiscent of European medieval or Gothic revival architecture, with decorative stonework and pointed arches.
description: A tall, stone Gothic-style tower with pointed spires, arched windows, and an attached hall with arched openings. The structure features a clock face and ornate stonework.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main tower with pointed spires, a large arched window, and the central stone facade.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the clock face, the attached hall with three arched openings, and a smaller turret at the end.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the arched doorway at the base and the symmetrical arrangement of spires and windows.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlights the clock face and the attached hall, mirroring the features seen in view 1.
